node.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Symmetric Tree and Subarray with atmost k odd elements

topic.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Symmetric Tree and Subarray with atmost k odd elements

post.content:
1. Given a binary tree, check whether it is a mirror of itself.
     https://leetcode.com/problems/symmetric-tree/
	 
2. Given an array find the number of distinct subarrays with atmost k odd elements
     https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/278341/Uber-or-Phone-Screen-or-Distinct-subarrays-with-at-most-k-odd-elements

Please suggest any problem in this solution (if any) (O(n) solution)
int start=0;
int last=0;
int ans=0;

Go as far to the right and stop when you find the (k+1)th odd element, Suppose at last=i. So basically all the sub-Arrays in the interval [start,last-1] will have at-most k odd elements.
Add the total sub-arrays possible in that range to the ans.
Now shrink the start to the point where you find the first odd element. Hence odd elements in the window [newstart, last] is k.
Repeat step 1.
Now there be might be a overlapping area between [start, last-1] and [newstart, newlast-1]. Hence indicating that we added all the sub-arrays in that range twice so we can remove all the sub-arrays o the overlapping range.

Example:
A=[1, 3, 9, 5], k = 2

Acc. to step 1 keep start on 0th index and take last to 1st index.( As A[0,2] includes k+1 odd elements)
ans=ans+3( Adding all sub-arrays of the range[0,1]);
ans=3

Move start to 1 and last to 2nd index( As [1,3] includes k+1 odd elements)
ans=ans+3 (Adding all sub-Arrays of the range [1,2])
ans=6

But, there is an overlap in the range [0,1] and [1,2] that is [1,1] so remove the sub-arrays of this range and proceed.
ans=5

Similarly you add 3 to the answer for [2,3] and subtract 1 from the answer for the overlapping range [2,2].
total ans=5+3-1
=7
